channel.title: Open-Xchange — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Open-Xchange (ox).

item.title: CVE-2026-42389 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Reject more queries with invalid header values
item.description: This fix provides extra hardening for the 5.4.x branch by doing extra validation of incoming answers from authoritative servers.

item.title: CVE-2026-52690 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Spoofed answers can mark an authoritative non-EDNS capable
item.description: Spoofing replies to Recursor might mark an IP of an authoritative server as not supporting EDNS, causing valdiation of DNSSEC records served by that server to fail.

item.title: CVE-2026-42390 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — ZONEMD validation can be bypassed
item.description: An invalid zone might pass ZONEMD validation while it should not. This is only relevant if ZoneToCache is configured with ZONEMD validation.

item.title: CVE-2026-42388 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Missing input validation for catalog zones
item.description: Incomplete validation of the SOA record present in a catalog zone might lead to a crash.

item.title: CVE-2026-42387 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Insufficient input validation in ZoneToCache
item.description: A malicious authoritative server can send a crafted zone via the ZoneToCache function that leads to a crash of the Recursor due to insuffcient input validation.

item.title: CVE-2026-40012 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Information about ECS zero scoped answers might leak to clients that use a specific ECS
item.description: ECS zero scoped answers are stored in the packet cache while they should not. This impacts only configurations that have ECS enabled;

item.title: CVE-2026-33612 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — ZoneToCache can poison the cache
item.description: A malicious authoritative server can send a crafted zone via the ZoneToCache function that leads to cache poisoning.

item.title: CVE-2026-42004 · 3.7 LOW 🌩️ — EDNS options smuggling
item.description: An attacker can send a crafted EDNS OPT record that will be ignored by DNSdist’s filtering rules, but will be rewritten as a valid OPT record when EDNS Client Subnet is inserted, causing the backend to see the EDNS option(s) that DNSdist did not filt…

item.title: CVE-2026-40211 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Denial of service via crafted DoH3 queries
item.description: An attacker can send crafted DNS over HTTP/3 queries, triggering an exception that prevents some buffer from being freed right away. The buffer will be freed at the end of the QUIC connection, but on some setups it might be possible to open enough co…

item.title: CVE-2026-40210 · 4.8 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Out-of-bounds read in SetMacAddrAction
item.description: An out-of-bounds read might happen when SetMacAddrAction is used, potentially resulting in uninitialized memory being sent over the network or a crash.

item.title: CVE-2026-40209 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Denial of service via IXFR queries
item.description: An attacker might be able to cause outgoing TCP connections to backend to be stuck until a timeout occurs instead of being released immediately, by sending IXFR queries. This could be used to cause a denial of service if there is a limit to the numbe…

item.title: CVE-2026-40208 · 3.7 LOW 🌩️ — Denial of service via DoH3 queries
item.description: An attacker might be able to delay the processing of DoH3 queries by sending DoH3 GET queries with an invalid DATA frame.

item.title: CVE-2026-40011 · 3.7 LOW 🌩️ — Prometheus denial of service via crafted DNS queries
item.description: An attacker sending a large number of crafted DNS queries might be able to trigger a dynamic block being inserted with a value causing invalid output to be produced in the prometheus endpoint. The prometheus endpoint will then be rejected by the scra…

item.title: CVE-2026-42005 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Insufficient input validation of internal web server
item.description: An attacker can send a web request that causes unlimited memory allocation in the internal web server, leading to a denial of service. The internal web server is disabled by default.

item.title: CVE-2026-41999 · 4.8 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Incorrect Behaviour of Views with TCP PROXY Requests
item.description: Incorrect Behaviour of Views with TCP PROXY Requests

item.title: CVE-2026-42002 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Concurrency and locking defects in GSS-TSIG
item.description: Concurrency and locking defects in GSS-TSIG

item.title: CVE-2026-42001 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Insufficient Validation of Autoprimary SOA Queries
item.description: Insufficient Validation of Autoprimary SOA Queries

item.title: CVE-2026-42000 · 6.8 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Insufficient Validation of Names During AXFR
item.description: Insufficient Validation of Names During AXFR

item.title: CVE-2026-42396 · 4.9 MEDIUM — Insufficient Validation of Member Zone Data May Cause Catalog Zone Transfer to Fail
item.description: Insufficient Validation of Member Zone Data May Cause Catalog Zone Transfer to Fail

item.title: CVE-2026-42006 · 4.3 MEDIUM
item.description: An attacker can cause uncontrolled memory usage with excessive bracing over IMAP. The fix in CVE-2026-27857 was incomplete, only blocking one way of doing this, so there was still another way left open. In particular, the fix was for closing braces,…

item.title: CVE-2026-40020 · 3.1 LOW
item.description: Attacker can use the IMAP SETACL command to inject the anyone permission to user's dovecot-acl file even if imap_acl_allow_anyone=no. This causes folders to be spammed to all users. The impact is limited to being able to spam folders to other users,…

item.title: CVE-2026-40016 · 5.3 MEDIUM
item.description: Attacker can upload a malicious Sieve script over ManageSieve service (or locally) to bypass configured CPU time limits for Sieve up to 130 times of the configured limit. Attacker can use this to degrade server performance and bypass configured CPU t…

item.title: CVE-2026-33603 · 6.8 MEDIUM
item.description: Attacker can use a specially crafted base64 exchange between Dovecot and Client to fake SCRAM TLS channel binding. This requires that the attacker is able to position itself between Dovecot and the client connection. If successful, the attacker can e…

item.title: CVE-2026-27851 · 7.4 HIGH 🌩️
item.description: When safe filter is used with variable expansion, all following pipelines on the same string are incorrectly interpreted as safe too, enabling unsafe data to be unescaped. This can enable SQL / LDAP injection attacks when used in authentication. Avoi…

item.title: CVE-2026-33611 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Insufficient validation of HTTPS and SVCB records
item.description: An operator allowed to use the REST API can cause the Authoritative server to produce invalid HTTPS or SVCB record data, which can in turn cause LMDB database corruption, if using the LMDB backend.

item.title: CVE-2026-33610 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Possible file descriptor exhaustion in forward-dnsupdate
item.description: A rogue primary server may cause file descriptor exhaustion and eventually a denial of service, when a PowerDNS secondary server forwards a DNS update request to it.

item.title: CVE-2026-33609 · 5.3 MEDIUM — LDAP DN injection
item.description: Incomplete escaping of LDAP queries when running with 8bit-dns enabled allows users to perform queries of internal domain subtrees.

item.title: CVE-2026-33608 · 7.4 HIGH 🌩️ — Incomplete domain name sanitization during
item.description: An attacker can send a notify request that causes a new secondary domain to be added to the bind backend, but causes said backend to update its configuration to an invalid one, leading to the backend no longer able to run on the next restart, requiri…

item.title: CVE-2026-33593 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Denial of service via crafted DNSCrypt query
item.description: A client can trigger a divide by zero error leading to crash by sending a crafted DNSCrypt query.

item.title: CVE-2026-33594 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Outgoing DoH excessive memory allocation
item.description: A client can trigger excessive memory allocation by generating a lot of queries that are routed to an overloaded DoH backend, causing queries to accumulate into a buffer that will not be released until the end of the connection.

item.title: CVE-2026-33595 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — DoQ/DoH3 excessive memory allocation
item.description: A client can trigger excessive memory allocation by generating a lot of errors responses over a single DoQ and DoH3 connection, as some resources were not properly released until the end of the connection.

item.title: CVE-2026-33597 · 3.7 LOW 🌩️ — PRSD detection denial of service
item.description: PRSD detection denial of service

item.title: CVE-2026-33596 · 3.1 LOW — TCP backend stream ID overflow
item.description: A client might theoretically be able to cause a mismatch between queries sent to a backend and the received responses by sending a flood of perfectly timed queries that are routed to a TCP-only or DNS over TLS backend.

item.title: CVE-2026-33598 · 4.8 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Out-of-bounds read in cache inspection via Lua
item.description: A cached crafted response can cause an out-of-bounds read if custom Lua code calls getDomainListByAddress() or getAddressListByDomain() on a packet cache.

item.title: CVE-2026-33599 · 3.1 LOW — Out-of-bounds read in service discovery
item.description: A rogue backend can send a crafted SVCB response to a Discovery of Designated Resolvers request, when requested via either the autoUpgrade (Lua) option to newServer or auto_upgrade (YAML) settings. DDR upgrade is not enabled by default.

item.title: CVE-2026-33602 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Off-by-one access when processing crafted UDP responses
item.description: A rogue backend can send a crafted UDP response with a query ID off by one related to the maximum configured value, triggering an out-of-bounds write leading to a denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2026-33254 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Resource exhaustion via DoQ/DoH3 connections
item.description: An attacker can create a large number of concurrent DoQ or DoH3 connections, causing unlimited memory allocation in DNSdist and leading to a denial of service. DOQ and DoH3 are disabled by default.

item.title: CVE-2026-33262 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Insufficient validation of cookie reply
item.description: An attacker can send replies that result in a null pointer dereference, caused by a missing consistency check and leading to a denial of service. Cookies are disabled by default.

item.title: CVE-2026-33261 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Null pointer accces in aggressive NSEC(3) cache
item.description: A zone transition from NSEC to NSEC3 might trigger an internal inconsistency and cause a denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2026-33260 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Insufficient input validation of internal webserver
item.description: An attacker can send a web request that causes unlimited memory allocation in the internal web server, leading to a denial of service. The internal web server is disabled by default.

item.title: CVE-2026-33259 · 5 MEDIUM — Concurrent modification of RPZ data can lead to denial of servce
item.description: Having many concurrent transfers of the same RPZ can lead to inconsistent RPZ data, use after free and/or a crash of the recursor. Normally concurrent transfers of the same RPZ zone can only occur with a malfunctioning RPZ provider.

item.title: CVE-2026-33258 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Crafted zones can cause increased resource usage
item.description: By publishing and querying a crafted zone an attacker can cause allocation of large entries in the negative and aggressive NSEC(3) caches.

item.title: CVE-2026-33257 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Insufficient input validation of internal webserver
item.description: An attacker can send a web request that causes unlimited memory allocation in the internal web server, leading to a denial of service. The internal web server is disabled by default.

item.title: CVE-2026-33256 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Unbounded memory allocation by internal web server
item.description: An attacker can send a web request that causes unlimited memory allocation in the internal web server, leading to a denial of service. The internal web server is disabled by default.

item.title: CVE-2026-33601 · 4.4 MEDIUM — Insufficient validation of zonemd record
item.description: If you use the zoneToCache function with a malicious authoritative server, an attacker can send a zone that result in a null pointer dereference, caused by a missing consistency check and leading to a denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2026-33600 · 4.4 MEDIUM — Null pointer dereference in RPZ transfer
item.description: An RPZ sent by a malicious authoritative server can result in a null pointer dereference, caused by a missing consistency check and leading to a denial of service.

item.title: CVE-2026-27854 · 4.8 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Use after free when parsing EDNS options in Lua
item.description: An attacker might be able to trigger a use-after-free by sending crafted DNS queries to a DNSdist using the DNSQuestion:getEDNSOptions method in custom Lua code. In some cases DNSQuestion:getEDNSOptions might refer to a version of the DNS packet that…

item.title: CVE-2026-27853 · 5.9 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Out-of-bounds write when rewriting large DNS packets
item.description: An attacker might be able to trigger an out-of-bounds write by sending crafted DNS responses to a DNSdist using the DNSQuestion:changeName or DNSResponse:changeName methods in custom Lua code. In some cases the rewritten packet might become larger th…

item.title: CVE-2026-24030 · 5.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Unbounded memory allocation for DoQ and DoH3
item.description: An attacker might be able to trick DNSdist into allocating too much memory while processing DNS over QUIC or DNS over HTTP/3 payloads, resulting in a denial of service. In setups with a large quantity of memory available this usually results in an ex…

item.title: CVE-2026-24029 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — DNS over HTTPS ACL bypass
item.description: When the early_acl_drop (earlyACLDrop in Lua) option is disabled (default is enabled) on a DNS over HTTPs frontend using the nghttp2 provider, the ACL check is skipped, allowing all clients to send DoH queries regardless of the configured ACL.
